rows.bis11: Everyday impulsive habits (acting fast without thinking, attention slips, weak planning).
rows.iat: Problematic internet use (internet use that starts causing problems).
rows.tmd: Mobile phone dependence (phone use that feels hard to control).
rows.eat26: Food and eating risk signals (possible unhealthy eating pattern signs).
rows.cbs: Compulsive buying (strong shopping urges and loss of control).

rows.bis11: Answer 30 self-report questions; points are added into totals and parts.
rows.iat: Answer 20 questions scored 0-5; total score is placed in severity bands.
rows.tmd: Answer 12 questions; score >=22 counted as dependence in this study.
rows.eat26: Answer 26 questions; score >=20 counted as elevated concern.
rows.cbs: Answer core shopping questions plus follow-ups; score < -1.34 counted as positive in this setup.

rows.bis11: Main impulsivity score: ANOVA (three-group average test) and t-tests inside OCD.
rows.iat: Used for both yes/no prevalence and severity score comparison.
rows.tmd: Yes/no prevalence was similar, but severity scores were higher in OCD (Mann-Whitney rank test).
rows.eat26: Yes/no prevalence looked similar, but severity scores were higher in OCD.
rows.cbs: Yes/no prevalence looked similar; severity difference was close (p = 0.055) but not below 0.05.
